skill.name: image-edit
skill.description: Routes image editing requests to RunComfy models for batch edits, text rewrites, reference composition, local edits, and mask-based inpainting.
skill.summary: Choose the right RunComfy image edit model for each request.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows most are markdown code spans, placeholder URLs, or textual false positives. The confirmed risk is legitimate but elevated: the skill instructs agents to call the RunComfy CLI, send prompts and image URLs to RunComfy services, use a token, and download generated files.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill directs agents to run RunComfy CLI commands. The command target is documented and appears legitimate, but it executes an external tool and sends request data to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command usage is explicit and repeated in the skill. The documented purpose is image editing, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image and Prompt Processing
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill sends prompts, image URLs, and mask URLs to RunComfy model services and downloads generated outputs. Users may expose sensitive images or prompt content to an external provider.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The security section states that the CLI posts to the Model API and fetches generated results. This confirms network transfer, but the endpoints match the stated service.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy Token Storage and Environment Use
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: The skill requires RunComfy login or RUNCOMFY_TOKEN. It documents local token storage with owner-only permissions, but marketplace users should understand credential handling before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token storage path and environment variable are explicitly documented. The file permission note lowers severity, but credential use still requires user awareness.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Reported as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer treated markdown inline code and fenced command examples as Ruby backtick execution. No Ruby source file or executable Ruby backtick expression is present in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown prose, tables, and shell examples. I found no evidence of Ruby code execution in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Placeholder URLs Reported as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The path traversal findings come from example URL placeholders containing ellipses. The examples are not filesystem paths and do not instruct traversal of local directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear inside HTTPS sample values such as image and mask URLs. No evidence found of local path traversal logic.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Textual
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary markdown text or model names, not cryptographic API calls. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, or other weak algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are frontmatter description, a route note, and an exit-code heading. They do not contain cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs Are Expected
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs point to RunComfy documentation, catalog pages, sample HTTPS inputs, or documented service endpoints. They align with the skill purpose and do not show covert callbacks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible and service-related. No evidence found of unrelated webhook, paste, tracking, or credential exfiltration endpoints.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses runcomfy run commands as its primary execution path. This is expected for the skill, but hosts should treat it as an external command capability.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit examples in SKILL.md. They are not hidden or obfuscated, but they do execute an external CLI.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Model API Transfer Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents that the CLI posts to the Model API, polls the request, and downloads results from RunComfy domains.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data flow is directly described in the security and operation notes. It confirms external network handling by design.

content.value_statement: Choosing the wrong image edit model wastes time and credits. This skill routes each request to a suitable RunComfy model and includes focused prompting patterns.
actual_capabilities: Routes general and batch image edits to Nano Banana Edit.
Routes multilingual text rewrites and multi-reference composition to GPT Image 2 Edit.
Routes single-reference local edits to Flux Kontext Pro.
Routes mask-based object removal and region replacement to Z-Image Turbo Inpaint.
Provides model-specific input fields, prompt patterns, and RunComfy CLI examples.
Documents token setup, output directories, exit codes, and RunComfy network endpoints.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a RunComfy account or token.
Sends prompts and referenced image URLs to RunComfy model services.
Chooses one model per call and does not blend multiple routes.
Depends on each model limit, including input count, size, mask, and reference constraints.

use_cases.title: Product Gallery Updates
use_cases.description: Batch edit product images with locked aspect ratio and resolution while preserving subjects and brand details.

use_cases.title: Localized Campaign Assets
use_cases.description: Rewrite in-image headlines in multiple languages while preserving layout, brand marks, and visual identity.

use_cases.title: Precise Retouching
use_cases.description: Remove objects or replace masked regions using model routes designed for local edits and inpainting.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Background Swap
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single image edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this image. Keep the subject identity, pose, and clothing unchanged. Replace the background with a clean studio setting.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Product Variant
prompt_templates.scenario: Consistent edits across several product images
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit these product images as a consistent set. Keep product shape, logo, and color unchanged. Use a white background and square output.

prompt_templates.title: Localized Poster Rewrite
prompt_templates.scenario: In-image text replacement with layout preservation
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace only the headline text with the translated phrase I provide. Keep the photograph, layout, font weight, and brand mark unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Masked Region Replacement
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced inpainting request with a mask URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mask to replace only the selected background. Preserve all unmasked objects, match the lighting, and use a high replacement strength.

output_examples.input: A user asks to remove overhead cables from a street photo and provides an image URL plus a mask URL.
output_examples.output: The skill selects the inpainting route, requests a grayscale mask, recommends moderate strength, and prepares the RunComfy edit request.

output_examples.input: A user asks to translate a poster headline into Japanese while keeping the same design.
output_examples.output: The skill selects GPT Image 2 Edit, preserves the layout and brand mark, and focuses the prompt on exact text replacement.

output_examples.input: A user provides ten product photos and asks for a consistent square catalog set.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Nano Banana Edit, locks aspect ratio and resolution, and prioritizes product identity preservation.

best_practices: State what must stay unchanged before describing the edit.
Use masks for precise removals, replacements, and background changes.
Lock aspect ratio and resolution when editing a batch.
anti_patterns: Do not send confidential images unless RunComfy processing is approved.
Do not combine unrelated edits in one prompt when a staged workflow is safer.
Do not use the inpainting route without a clear grayscale mask.

faq.question: Does this skill edit images locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the local RunComfy CLI, which sends requests to RunComfy model services.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it process batches?
faq.answer: Yes. The Nano Banana Edit route supports batches up to the model limit documented in the skill.

faq.question: Can it rewrite text inside images?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill routes multilingual in-image text rewrite requests to GPT Image 2 Edit.

faq.question: Does it need a mask for object removal?
faq.answer: Only the Z-Image Turbo Inpaint route requires a grayscale mask URL.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: Prompts, image URLs, masks, and generated outputs are handled through RunComfy services and the RunComfy CLI.
